title: Richly Romantic Malaysian Chicken Satay
description: A fatty and crisp Malaysian dish featuring black treacle, this recipe is perfect for a romantic evening, with an ayurvedic-rajasic friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit to dehydrate the chicken.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut milk, black treacle, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and salt.
Add the chicken strips to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated chicken onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush with peanut oil.
Dehydrate the chicken in the oven for 20 minutes, or until slightly charred.
Increase the oven temperature to broil and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until crispy.
Serve the satay hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Offer a side of peanut sauce for dipping, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
